planning_gate.spec_delta: M19 now tests targeted knee/hip pose residual and terminal stand recovery inside exp83's safe 8cm multi-step stance guard corridor.

perspectives.product: focuses on the visible pose gap that blocks the first no-fall 8cm/contact/slip run
perspectives.architecture: monkeypatches exp67 WBC-lite candidate selection with target-level pose bias and horizon pose shortfall scoring
perspectives.security: no credentials or external side effects
perspectives.qa: native sweep records raw JSON, pose shortfall, and visible gate metrics per pose-bias variant
perspectives.skeptic: knee/hip bias may recreate the depth-biased fall branch or sacrifice terminal return

dod: raw native JSON per horizon variant
summary states whether any variant passes visible_8cm_gate

sources.note: Official G1 page lists 23-43 joint motors, 6 DoF per leg, knee torque up to 90/120 N.m, and knee/hip joint ranges compatible with squat geometry.
G1 operation docs expose Squat Mode but mark it as no balance control, matching the need for a controlled stance-aware policy rather than a posture toggle.
HOMIE reports humanoid policies that walk and squat to specific heights using height tracking reward and RL.
HuB validates extreme balance tasks on Unitree G1 and includes Deep Squat in its evaluation set.
